title: Router Decision
description: Structured output of the deterministic message router (KÖR-6a, working name OpenClaw Router per docs/heavy-llm-flow/02). Classifies a single user message into messageKind + buildRequirement + contextLevel + target/subtasks so pure questions, discovery and external references never trigger a build, adapter or iframe. This is NOT a persisted canonical run-artefakt: the router returns the decision in-memory and may only append it to an existing run's trace.ndjson. The shape mirrors packages.generation.orchestration.router.models.RouterDecision; tests/test_router_schema.py locks the two against drift (top-level + nested $defs).

messageKind.description: What kind of message this is (02 §2.1).

editKind.description: The concrete edit, when the message is an edit. 'none' for non-edit kinds.

buildRequirement.description: How much the system must do (02 §2.2). 'none'/'plan_only' must never start a build or preview.

contextLevel.description: How much context a downstream handler should assemble (02 §4).

target.description: Where an edit applies. Null when there is no concrete target.

subtasks.description: Ordered deluppgifter for a multi_intent decision; empty otherwise.

reference.description: External reference the user pointed at; null when none.

componentIntent.description: Best-effort component slug for a component edit (e.g. 'clock_widget').

rationale.description: Short explanation of why this decision was made, including why a build was (not) required.

requiresClarification.description: True when the router cannot act and should ask the user to clarify.

shouldStartPreview.description: The only actuation flag the router owns. True only for a real rebuild and forced false under builder coexistence.
